answers.answer: The file should end with a trailing newline. No .editorconfig exists in the project, but standard POSIX text file convention and git best practices expect files to end with a newline. The project's .gitignore has no special rules for .txt files. Using a trailing newline avoids git diff noise ('No newline at end of file' warning).
answers.uncertainty: The original task description doesn't specify exact content format, but trailing newline is the safe default.

assumption_checks.impact: frontend/ directory exists and contains project files (index.html, package.json, src/, etc.). No action needed.
No project-level configuration (editorconfig, linter rules) specifies otherwise. Standard trailing newline convention applies.
The .gitignore only excludes __pycache__/, node_modules/, dist/ (with !frontend/dist/ exception), .dolt/, *.db, and .beads-credential-key. No rules exclude .txt files from any directory.
